name: Replace a Lost or Stolen Card and Expedite Delivery
description: Call the card issuer to (a) immediately lock or cancel a lost or stolen card, (b) order a replacement with the correct shipping speed, and (c) deal with the gap-period plumbing — recurring charges, push-provisioning to mobile wallets, virtual card numbers.
disclaimer: Not financial or tax advice. Agent must NEVER share SSN unprompted, NEVER initiate wires above the operator's stated cap, NEVER agree to a hardship plan / settlement that affects credit without explicit ask_operator approval.

context.when_to_use: Card is missing (left in a cab, stolen wallet, lost in luggage). Speed matters: each hour increases fraud-loss exposure even though Reg E / Reg Z cap user liability. Mid-trip card-loss has the extra wrinkle that the user may need expedited international delivery or a temporary virtual card to keep functioning.
preconditions: User can confirm: physically missing vs definitely stolen (matters for fraud reporting).
User has the card account info (last 4 from statement / app).
User is calling from the number on the back of the card or the bank's mobile app.
User knows their current address and any temporary shipping address (e.g., hotel).
User has a list of recurring charges that were on the lost card (Netflix, gym, utilities — all need to be updated to the new number).

principles: Lock first, talk later. Many bank apps have a 'lock card' toggle that pauses authorizations instantly — use it BEFORE the phone call if there's any delay reaching a human.
Distinguish LOST from STOLEN. Stolen often triggers a fraud-report workflow even if there are no unauthorized charges yet; lost is a simple replace.

Ask for push-provisioning. Most modern issuers can push a digital card number to Apple Pay / Google Pay / Samsung Pay within minutes of the call, so the user can keep transacting before the physical card arrives.
Or ask for a VIRTUAL CARD NUMBER (VCN) — some issuers (Cap One Eno, Citi Virtual Card, etc.) generate a number you can use immediately online while waiting for plastic.
Expedited shipping is usually FREE if the card was lost/stolen — never pay $25 for FedEx Overnight if you ask politely.

Recurring charges break when the card number changes — unless the issuer participates in 'card account updater' programs. Some merchants get the new number automatically; some don't. Plan to manually update the rest.

phrases.opener: Hi, I need to report my card as [lost / stolen] and order a replacement. Before we do anything else, can you immediately lock or cancel the current card number?
phrases.lost_vs_stolen: To clarify: the card is [definitely stolen — my wallet was taken / missing — I don't believe it was stolen, I likely misplaced it]. Should this be filed as a fraud case or a simple replacement?
phrases.request_push_provisioning: While the physical card ships, can you push the new card number to my Apple Pay / Google Pay so I can use it immediately?
phrases.request_virtual_card: Does the card support a virtual card number I can use for online purchases right now while the plastic arrives?
phrases.expedited_shipping_ask: Given this is a [lost / stolen] situation, can you expedite shipping at no charge? When would I have the new card?
phrases.alternate_address: I'm currently at [hotel / temporary address] until [date]. Can the card ship there, and if so, what verification do you need at that address?
phrases.check_recurring_updater: Will my recurring merchants automatically receive the new card number via the network's account-updater service, or do I need to update each one manually? Can you give me a list of which merchants are covered?
phrases.graceful_close: Thanks. Can you confirm: old card cancelled, new card ordered with expedited delivery to [address], expected arrival [date], digital card pushed to wallet, virtual card number provided if applicable, and give me a reference number plus your name?

tactics.name: Lock first via the app
tactics.when: If the bank's app supports it.
tactics.script: Open the app, toggle 'lock card' or 'report missing' BEFORE the call. This stops new authorizations even if you wait on hold for 20 minutes.

tactics.name: Lock-on-call immediately
tactics.script: Use `opener`. Cancellation is irreversible — but the loss exposure from waiting longer is larger than the inconvenience of cancelling.

tactics.name: Classify lost vs stolen
tactics.script: Use `lost_vs_stolen`. Stolen + any unauthorized charges → also use `report-fraudulent-transaction` workflow.

tactics.name: Bridge the gap — push to wallet
tactics.script: Use `request_push_provisioning`. Apple Pay / Google Pay / Samsung Pay typically activate within minutes — the user can buy coffee before they walk out of the building.

tactics.name: Bridge the gap — virtual card
tactics.script: Use `request_virtual_card`. VCNs let the user pay online (or a hotel hold) right away.

tactics.name: Expedite for free
tactics.script: Use `expedited_shipping_ask`. 1-2 day shipping is usually free on lost/stolen replacements — they only charge it if you don't ask.

tactics.name: Handle shipping address logistics
tactics.when: User is traveling or has moved.
tactics.script: Use `alternate_address`. Bank may require verification (ID at delivery, secondary phone, security question) — ask what's required.

tactics.name: Map recurring updates
tactics.script: Use `check_recurring_updater`. Ask which networks (Visa Account Updater, Mastercard ABU) are subscribed AND which merchants are NOT — they need manual updating.

tactics.name: Audit trail
tactics.script: Use `graceful_close`. Confirmation of cancellation + replacement + delivery + digital tools + reference number.

boundaries: Do NOT cancel without a replacement ordered if the card is needed for recurring critical bills — coordinate timing.
Do NOT have the card shipped to an unverified third-party address — fraud risk; if user is traveling, use a hotel with the user's name on the reservation, or hold-at-FedEx.
Do NOT share full SSN; last 4 plus card info is sufficient.
Do NOT verify identity by reading back a one-time code the bank just texted (legitimate reps will never ask).
Do NOT close the call without reference number and an expected delivery date.
Do NOT pay for expedited shipping without asking for it free first.
success_signals: Old card number cancelled before the call ends.
New card ordered, expedited delivery free.
Digital card pushed to wallet within minutes.
Account-updater coverage understood for recurring merchants.
failure_signals: Rep refuses expedited shipping at no charge — escalate to supervisor or use the mobile-wallet push as the bridge.
Bank doesn't support push-provisioning AND user is traveling without backup card.
Alternate address can't be verified (escalate or use known-good address).
Account is flagged for suspended status — escalate to fraud/account services.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm reference number, new card delivery date, digital-wallet card active. Surface to operator the list of recurring merchants that need manual updating. Calendar to do that within 48 hours.
exit_strategy.on_failure: If delivery fails or address verification blocks: try in-branch pickup (some banks support this). If push-provisioning fails: use a backup card and accept the gap. If the account is suspended: escalate to a fraud supervisor and request a written summary of the suspension reason.
follow_ups: Day +1: confirm digital card works for a small test purchase.
Day +5: manually update each recurring merchant NOT covered by account-updater.
Day +30: pull statement to verify no zombie charges from the old card.

required_user_info: List of recurring merchants on this card
Backup payment method available during the gap
